title: Understanding Tangential Acceleration
text: The tangential acceleration is the rate of change of the tangential velocity of the tip of the blade. It is given as $$3.00$$ m/s².

title: Finding Tangential Velocity
text: To find the tangential velocity at $$t=5.0$$ s, use the formula: $$v=at$$, where a is the tangential acceleration and $$t$$ is the time.

title: Calculate Tangential Velocity
text: Calculate the tangential velocity at $$t=5.0$$ s using $$v=at$$.

title: Understanding Radial Acceleration
text: Radial acceleration (also known as centripetal acceleration) is given by the formula a_r=v²/r, where v is the tangential velocity and $$r$$ is the radius.

title: Calculate Radial Acceleration
text: Calculate the radial acceleration using a_r=v²/r.

title: Total Acceleration
text: The total acceleration is the vector sum of tangential and radial accelerations. Use the formula: a_total=sqrt(a_t²+a_r²).

title: Calculate Total Acceleration
text: Calculate the total acceleration using a_total=sqrt(a_t²+a_r²).
